description: URL of the falcon.json JSON Schema, enabling rule autocomplete and level validation in editors.
Master switch for cross-file analysis. Set to false to disable all cross-file rules. Defaults to true.
Enable the recommended preset for this section. When on, every rule in the preset reports at its default severity unless individually overridden. Defaults to true.
A rule's configuration: either a bare severity level, or an object with a `level` and rule-specific `options`.
type: rule level (or object with level + options)

description: Glob patterns for the files falcon analyzes. When omitted, every Dart file under the working directory is analyzed.
Activation level for the `flutter` domain's rules: `all` enables every rule in the domain, `recommended` only its recommended rules, `none` disables them.
Master switch for the single-file linter. Set to false to disable all file rules. Defaults to true.
Enable the recommended preset for this section. When on, every rule in the preset reports at its default severity unless individually overridden. Defaults to true.
A rule's configuration: either a bare severity level, or an object with a `level` and rule-specific `options`.
type: rule level (or object with level + options)

description: Stop after emitting this many diagnostics. Null (the default) means no limit.
Master switch for cross-file analysis within this override.
Enable the recommended preset for this section. When on, every rule in the preset reports at its default severity unless individually overridden. Defaults to true.
A rule's configuration: either a bare severity level, or an object with a `level` and rule-specific `options`.
type: rule level (or object with level + options)

description: Glob patterns selecting the files this override applies to.
Master switch for the single-file linter within this override.
Enable the recommended preset for this section. When on, every rule in the preset reports at its default severity unless individually overridden. Defaults to true.
A rule's configuration: either a bare severity level, or an object with a `level` and rule-specific `options`.
type: rule level (or object with level + options)
